brief_title: Roxadustat for Anemia of Chronic Kidney Disease in Non-Dialysis Patients
official_title: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study of Roxadustat (HIF-PHI) for the Treatment of Anemia in Non-Dialysis Patients with Chronic Kidney Disease
interventions: Roxadustat oral tablet three times weekly (dose titrated to target Hgb 10-12 g/dL)
Placebo oral tablet three times weekly
brief_summary: This Phase 2 study evaluates the efficacy and safety of roxadustat, a hypoxia-inducible factor prolyl hydroxylase inhibitor (HIF-PHI), for the treatment of anemia in non-dialysis patients with chronic kidney disease. The primary endpoint is change in hemoglobin from baseline to week 12.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Adults aged 18-80 years

2. Chronic kidney disease with eGFR 15-45 mL/min/1.73m² not on dialysis

3. Hemoglobin < 10.0 g/dL at screening (mean of two values)

4. Ferritin >= 30 ng/mL and TSAT >= 15%

5. Not currently on erythropoiesis-stimulating agents (ESA) or ESA discontinued >= 4 weeks prior

6. Folate and vitamin B12 levels within normal limits or adequately supplemented

7. Systolic blood pressure <= 170 mmHg at screening

8. No red blood cell transfusion within 4 weeks of screening

9. Written informed consent

10. Able to swallow oral tablets
raw_exclusion_text:
1. Expected to require dialysis within 3 months

2. Known cause of anemia other than CKD (e.g., hemolytic anemia, aplastic anemia, GI bleeding)

3. Active or history of macular edema or diabetic retinopathy requiring treatment

4. Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic > 170 mmHg or diastolic > 110 mmHg)

5. History of deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism within 12 months

6. History of seizure within past 12 months

7. Active malignancy (except non-melanoma skin cancer)

8. Pregnant or breastfeeding

9. Significant hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh B or C)

inclusion_criteria.text: Chronic kidney disease with eGFR 15-45 mL/min/1.73m² not on dialysis
Hemoglobin < 10.0 g/dL at screening (mean of two values)
Not currently on erythropoiesis-stimulating agents (ESA) or ESA discontinued >= 4 weeks prior
Folate and vitamin B12 levels within normal limits or adequately supplemented
Systolic blood pressure <= 170 mmHg at screening
No red blood cell transfusion within 4 weeks of screening

exclusion_criteria.text: Expected to require dialysis within 3 months
Known cause of anemia other than CKD (e.g., hemolytic anemia, aplastic anemia, GI bleeding)
Active or history of macular edema or diabetic retinopathy requiring treatment
Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic > 170 mmHg or diastolic > 110 mmHg)
History of deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism within 12 months
History of seizure within past 12 months
Significant hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh B or C)
